2020-09-24.abstract: Seven months since the Philippines enacted its first lockdown, Jolog’s Barbershop exemplifies the toll the coronavirus has exacted on the country in lost lives, income and sense of community.

2020-09-24.title: Even as Virus Surges, Hungary Forges Ahead With Super Cup Soccer Game
2020-09-24.abstract: Bayern Munich and Sevilla battled for the European trophy in Budapest despite growing concerns over fan safety.

2020-09-24.title: Night Images Reveal Many New Detention Sites in China’s Xinjiang Region
2020-09-24.abstract: China said it was winding down its “re-education” camps for Uighurs and other minorities, but researchers found evidence that incarceration is on the rise.

2020-09-24.title: Israel Tightens Holiday Lockdown, Restricting Prayers and Protests
2020-09-24.abstract: Israel now has one of the world’s worst outbreaks of the coronavirus, but the government’s response has gotten mired in political and cultural wars.

2020-09-25.title: Powerful Cardinal, a Fixture of Vatican Intrigue, Resigns Suddenly
2020-09-25.abstract: In a cryptic statement, the Vatican did not explain the reason for Cardinal Giovanni Angelo Becciu’s departure. News reports had connected him to a financial scandal.

2020-09-25.title: China Is Erasing Mosques and Precious Shrines in Xinjiang
2020-09-25.abstract: Thousands of religious sites in Xinjiang have been destroyed, a new analysis suggests, part of China’s drive to erode the region’s heritage.

2020-09-25.title: Kim Jong-un Offers South Korea Rare Apology for Killing of Official
2020-09-25.abstract: “I am deeply sorry that an unexpected and unfortunate thing has happened in our territorial waters,” the North Korean leader said in a message, according to officials in the South.

2020-09-25.title: Stranded Overseas, Thousands Beg Australia to Let Them Come Home
2020-09-25.abstract: Limits on the number of Australians who can return have spurred a growing uproar over the country’s hard-line approach to the coronavirus.

2020-09-25.title: Her Study Center Was Bombed. She Still Topped Afghanistan’s National University Exam.
2020-09-25.abstract: Shamsea Alizada’s story of persistence is a reminder of advances in girls’ education in Afghanistan — and their vulnerability as the government negotiates with the Taliban.

2020-09-26.title: This Carousel Has Had Quite a Ride. Will Anyone in Japan Save It?
2020-09-26.abstract: The 113-year-old merry-go-round, which arrived in Tokyo after stints in Germany and Coney Island, is now in storage, its fate uncertain in a country that tends to preserve only the very old.

2020-09-26.title: Pandemic Will ‘Take Our Women 10 Years Back’ in the Workplace
2020-09-26.abstract: Improvements in gender equality in the workplace may be another casualty of the coronavirus, as women find their place in the work force more at risk.

2020-09-26.title: In Melbourne, Springtime Ushers in a New Sense of Hope
2020-09-26.abstract: As our Covid-19 restrictions ease, the mood of the city feels like the weather.

2020-09-26.title: Leader of India Says Country’s Vaccine Industry Will ‘Help All Humanity’
2020-09-26.abstract: A study offered an explanation for children’s ability to fight off the virus. The lockdown in Melbourne, Australia, is being further eased.

2020-09-26.title: Tasmania Whale Rescue Ends With 108 Saved and Over 300 Dead
2020-09-26.abstract: The whale beaching, one of the largest strandings ever recorded globally, prompted a five-day rescue effort.

2020-09-27.title: Sudan Is Focus of U.S. Efforts to Improve Ties With Israel
2020-09-27.abstract: The U.S. is offering cash aid and promises to entice Sudan to recognize Israel before the American election, while the big prize, Saudi Arabia, remains out of reach.

2020-09-27.title: There’s Gold in Them Thar Braes
2020-09-27.abstract: With prices for the precious metal surging, amateur prospectors are fanning out over the Scottish countryside, while the nation’s first commercial mine is set to start production.

2020-09-27.title: Mine Disaster in China Kills at Least 16
2020-09-27.abstract: A fire led to excessively high levels of carbon monoxide, officials said. The site had previously been fined for safety violations.

2020-09-27.title: Fighting Flares Between Azerbaijan and Armenia
2020-09-27.abstract: The governments of both countries reported action with tanks, military helicopters and artillery in a rapid escalation of a long-simmering conflict.

2020-09-27.title: Top White House Official Denies Pressuring F.D.A. on Vaccine Guidelines
2020-09-27.abstract: The number of lives lost to the virus is nearing one million, and new hot spots keep emerging. The U.S., Brazil, India and Mexico account for more than half of the total.

2020-09-28.title: American Could Face Prison in Thailand After Posting Negative Reviews of a Resort
2020-09-28.abstract: The man was arrested under Thailand’s criminal defamation law, which has been used to silence critics and stifle dissent. The hotel that brought charges acknowledges that using the law might be “excessive.”

2020-09-28.title: Ex-President of Pakistan Is Indicted on Money Laundering Charges
2020-09-28.abstract: The case against Asif Ali Zardari, widower of former Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto, is the latest in a string of moves against opposition figures that his supporters say are politically motivated.

2020-09-28.title: Spanish Court Upholds Ban on Catalan Leader
2020-09-28.abstract: The decision barring the separatist president of Catalonia’s regional government, Quim Torra, from holding public office confirms a ruling in December and threatens to reignite unrest.

2020-09-28.title: Myanmar’s virus restrictions keep journalists at home.
2020-09-28.abstract: The strict stay-at-home order issued by Myanmar last week for its largest city, Yangon, barred residents from traveling from any of its 44 townships to another. There are some exceptions, such as for police officers, emergency workers and doctors.

2020-09-28.title: As Europe’s Coronavirus Cases Rise, So Do Voices Crying Hoax
2020-09-28.abstract: As health officials warn of a second major wave, growing numbers of people are dismissing the threat as media hype and sometimes embracing conspiracy theories.

2020-09-29.title: Coronavirus Deaths Pass One Million Worldwide
2020-09-29.abstract: Over the past 10 months, the virus has taken more lives than H.I.V., malaria, influenza and cholera. And as it sows destruction in daily life around the globe, it is still growing quickly.

2020-09-29.title: No More ‘Ladies and Gentlemen’ on Japan Airlines
2020-09-29.abstract: The carrier pledged to use inclusive language in a country where gender roles are entrenched.

2020-09-29.title: For Hong Kong Protesters Caught at Sea, Trial in China Is Likely
2020-09-29.abstract: Detained by the Chinese Coast Guard as they tried to flee the city, the activists are now in the hands of the mainland’s opaque criminal justice system.

2020-09-29.title: The pandemic is far from over, experts say, despite Trump allies’ claims.
2020-09-29.abstract: In the past two weeks, leading epidemiologists from many respected institutions have, through different methods, reached the same conclusion: About 85 to 90 percent of the American population is still susceptible to SARS-CoV-2, the virus causing the current pandemic.

2020-09-29.title: Vilified Early Over Lax Virus Strategy, Sweden Seems to Have Scourge Controlled
2020-09-29.abstract: After having weathered high death rates when it resisted a lockdown in the spring, Sweden now has one of Europe’s lowest rates of daily new cases. Whether that is an aberration remains to be seen.

2020-09-30.title: Teacher in China Is Sentenced to Death for Poisoning 25 Students
2020-09-30.abstract: The teacher put nitrite in the kindergarten students’ breakfast porridge, poisoning 25 and killing one.

2020-09-30.title: Abandoned Rio Tinto Mine Is Blamed for Poisoned Bougainville Rivers
2020-09-30.abstract: Residents of the Papua New Guinea region have accused the mining giant of environmental and human rights violations and asked for an investigation.

2020-09-30.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix, Amazon and Stan in Australia in October
2020-09-30.abstract: Our streaming picks for October, including ‘Rebecca,’ ‘The Haunting of Bly Manor,’ and ‘Welcome to the Blumhouse’

2020-09-30.title: In Madrid, Covid-19 Resurgence Divides Rich and Poor
2020-09-30.abstract: New lockdown measures disproportionately affect the most economically vulnerable people in the capital region.

2020-09-30.title: The pandemic’s financial pain is worst for Black and Latino parents, a survey finds.
2020-09-30.abstract: More than 60 percent of households with children in the United States reported serious financial problems — including struggles to afford medical care, depletion of household savings and difficulty paying credit card and other debts — during the coronavirus pandemic, according to a new poll.
